description: An integrated approach for using geomatics (remote sensing, GIS, spatial analysis) for data acquisition, processing and analysis to improve our understanding of marine and coastal areas. Topics focus on applications of geomatics for environmental and social lab work and seminar discussions. Students should be prepared to undertake one or more weekend field trips; a fee for each field trip may be applied.
preAndCorequisites:
• Complete all of the following
    • Complete all of:
      • GEOG319 - Remote Sensing of the Environment using Passive Sensors (1.5)
    • Complete 1 of:
      • GEOG328 - GIS Analysis (1.5)
      • GEOG329 - GIS Applications and Tools (1.5)
recommendations: • GEOG 252 and one of GEOG 370, GEOG 373, GEOG 376 recommended prior to GEOG 420.
title: Field Studies in Coastal Geomatics
